title: Review & setlist: Charlie Puth turns MGM Music Hall into a masterclass in musicianship
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Charlie Puth performed at MGM Music Hall in Fenway on May 22, mixing jazz and pop influences.
The concert featured songs from his March album 'Whatever’s Clever!', alongside older hits that excited the audience.
Openers Ally Salort and Daniel Seavey showcased their musical talents, preparing the crowd for Puth's performance.

title: Review and setlist: Jakob Dylan’s choice to channel Tom Petty pays off at Beverly Wallflowers show
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: The Wallflowers performed 'Bringing Down the Horse' and Tom Petty's 'Long After Dark' at The Cabot, May 22, 2026.
Jakob Dylan, 56, led the band through hits like 'One Headlight' and Petty covers, displaying a more gruff vocal tone.
The setlist included an encore with 'Refugee' and 'The Waiting', leaving a joyful audience at the sold-out Beverly venue.

title: Here’s where people are moving — and leaving — in Mass., according to new U.S. Census data
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston lost 1,338 residents, marking the largest population decline in Massachusetts last year, with a total population of 672,973.
Everett and Worcester saw the largest population gains, adding 2,362 and 1,916 residents respectively, according to U.S. Census data.
Massachusetts population growth slowed to 0.2 percent last year, impacted by high housing costs and reduced immigration.

title: These were the Mass. companies on the new Forbes list of largest family businesses
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Fidelity Investments, controlled by the Johnson family, ranked No. 14 on Forbes' list of largest U.S. family businesses.
Tewksbury-based Demoulas Super Markets, owned by the Demoulas family, ranked No. 69 on Forbes' list, earning $8 billion annually.
The Forbes list ranks multigenerational family businesses based on revenue, including those with significant family ownership and leadership.

title: UMass Amherst launches major housing revitalization project
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: UMass Amherst partners with American Campus Communities for a long-term campus housing overhaul.
The project aims to modernize student housing, expand amenities, and maintain affordability.
Redevelopment will occur in phases, aligning with enrollment growth and housing studies.

title: Review & Setlist: Lucinda Williams brings protest songs, fighting spirit to Medford
author: Terence Cawley
bullets: Lucinda Williams performed at Chevalier Theatre in Medford, featuring songs from her new album 'World's Gone Wrong'.
Williams emphasized protest themes, performing eight out of nine songs from recent politically motivated albums.
The setlist included Bob Marley, Memphis Minnie, and Neil Young covers, closing with 'Rockin' in the Free World'.

title: Twins unload on Justin Slaten as Red Sox lose 8-6 at Fenway Park
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Justin Slaten gave up four runs, including two home runs, in the seventh inning against the Twins.
Red Sox starter Payton Tolle had nine strikeouts but was pulled after six innings at 85 pitches.
The Red Sox lost 8-6 to the Twins, despite scoring six runs, a rare occurrence this season.

title: Roger Clemens throws out first pitch at Fenway Park to son, Twins player Kody Clemens
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Roger Clemens threw the ceremonial first pitch at Fenway Park to his son, Kody Clemens, a Minnesota Twins player.
Clemens sported a custom Red Sox jersey and later met fans, signing autographs before the game.
Roger Clemens was honored with a bobblehead giveaway, celebrating his historic achievements with the Boston Red Sox.

title: Celtics’ Derrick White named to 2025-26 NBA All-Defensive First Team
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Derrick White named to the NBA All-Defensive First Team for the 2025-26 season with 58 votes.
White recorded a career-high 98 blocks and 88 steals, leading all guards with 552 contested shots.
Boston Celtics finished second in the Eastern Conference with a 56-26 record, largely without Jayson Tatum.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Maximum Pleasure Guaranteed' starring Tatiana Maslany is now streaming on Apple TV.
Oscar-winning film 'Glory' about the 54th Massachusetts Infantry Regiment is available on Netflix until June 1.
New movies such as 'Arco' and shows like 'The Mandalorian' and 'Strangers with Candy' are streaming on various platforms.
